title: Concurrency, Cancellation, Timeouts, Retries, and Flake Control · Independent Delivery Trial
summary: An independent delivery team receives an unfamiliar repository, event, runner, and trust constraint and must transfer concurrency, cancellation, timeouts, retries, and flake control into a new bounded execution and concurrency policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of bounded execution and concurrency policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Build concurrency keys from workflow, branch, pull request, service, or environment identity and choose cancellation from business semantics.
Prevent stale runs and out-of-order artifacts from overwriting newer deployments through revision, environment, and compare-before-mutate checks.
Set bounded job, step, command, network, and shutdown timeouts while terminating child processes and retaining partial evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Ci Concurrency Group Cancel Policy
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab older deployment finishes after a newer revision, cancellation skips cleanup, a child process exceeds 4 minutes, and retries erase the first failure. Bound concurrency, staleness, timeouts, idempotency, and flake repair.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Ci Concurrency Group Cancel Policy.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for build concurrency keys from workflow, branch, pull request, service, or environment identity and choose cancellation from business semantics. steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-flake-detection-quarantine-repair-7komin". Tailor its execution-control.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair, keep Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-flake-detection-quarantine-repair-7komin
  - competency: ci-flake-detection-quarantine-repair
    artifact: execution-control.yaml
    decision: flake detection quarantine repair
    environment: incident replay using redacted run metadata and immutable artifacts
    fault-injection: cancel an older run while its external mutation is in progress
    retained-proof: lock hash cold install test report package contents and output digest
    case-key: 7komin
    change: |
      concurrency-group: deploy-production-service-a
      cancel-in-progress: tests-only
      revision-guard: compare-before-mutate
      job-timeout-minutes: 20
      command-timeout-seconds: 90
      cleanup: always-idempotent
      retry: classified-transient-max-2
      first-failure: retained
      flake-owner-expiry: team-delivery-7-days
    verify: simulate overlapping revisions cancellation timeout transient failure duplicate side effect and cleanup interruption
    failure: Reject blanket cancellation, commit-order assumptions, timeout as cleanup, all-error retry, or ownerless quarantine.
    repair: Scope concurrency to shared state, reject stale mutation, bound every wait, preserve first failure, and expire quarantine.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair structural check is only one gate. bounded execution and concurrency policy must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

At Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair, trace this concrete input and predict evaluation, shell, job graph, cache, artifact, trust, deployment, signal, rollback, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A lab older deployment finishes after a newer revision, cancellation skips cleanup, a child process exceeds 2 minutes, and retries erase the first failure. Bound concurrency, staleness, timeouts, idempotency, and flake repair. Competency probe: Detect nondeterminism through repeated changed schedules, classify cause, quarantine with owner and expiry, and require repaired regression evidence.

For Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: A flaky check should be removed from required status until someone has time to investigate.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair output and hide the violated bounded execution and concurrency policy assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair.

For test an acceptance boundary, copy one familiar workflow, omit event and revision identity, permissions, runner, immutable artifact, failure, rollback, and disposable delivery-environment transfer checks, then accept a green run as production proof. This does not satisfy Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair for bounded execution and concurrency policy.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab older deployment finishes after a newer revision, cancellation skips cleanup, a child process exceeds 3 minutes, and retries erase the first failure. Bound concurrency, staleness, timeouts, idempotency, and flake repair. Competency probe: Detect nondeterminism through repeated changed schedules, classify cause, quarantine with owner and expiry, and require repaired regression evidence.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair.
steps.why: Retrieving Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair through the concrete bounded execution and concurrency policy case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair, keep Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Detect nondeterminism through repeated changed schedules, classify cause, quarantine with owner and expiry, and require repaired regression evidence.
